text.title: agentic-rag Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for agentic-rag as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Infinite rewrite loop: poorly graded relevance causes the agent to retry indefinitely without converging.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each rewrite degrades from original intent; after 3+ rewrites, the agent may search for something unrelated to the user's question. Infinite loops: a poorly graded relevance check can cause the agent to retry indefinitely without converging.

failure-mode.description: Grader LLM hallucination: the relevance grader marks a document relevant because it shares topic vocabulary, not because it answers the query.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM-based graders have the same hallucination risks as generation models. A document about "Python async" may score relevant for "Python async error" even if it contains no error information. Relevance grader hallucination: the grader LLM may incorrectly mark relevant documents as irrelevant, silently dropping good context.

failure-mode.description: State corruption in multi-agent coordination: if one agent crashes mid-loop and state is not persisted, the orchestrator restarts with partial context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In-memory state is lost on crash. Production pipelines must checkpoint agent state to durable storage (PostgreSQL, Redis) between hops.

failure-mode.description: Response verifier treated as a guarantee: verifier LLM itself can fail to detect hallucinations if the hallucinated content is plausibly worded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat verifier output as a probabilistic signal, not a fact-check. For high-stakes domains, add a human review checkpoint before returning the final answer. Response verifier is itself an LLM: it can fail to detect hallucinations if the hallucinated content is plausibly worded — treat verifier output as a probabilistic signal, not a guarantee.
